title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Spencer presents a deceased man, claiming to be Enoch Yates, to Rosalyn Claude. Rosalyn denies ever knowing the man, prompting Spencer to privately suspect she is a skilled actress. Spencer informs Mr. Claud that the widow's testimony should settle the matter with the jury, though Mr. Claud remains skeptical.

Hercule Poirot, having read a newspaper report about the death of Enoch Yates in Wormsley Valley, becomes interested in the case. The report notes that forensic evidence shows the man died from skull fractures and that the police are seeking information regarding Yates, who reportedly arrived from Cape Town. Poirot recalls a conversation involving Major Porter and a recent visit from Mrs. Leonie Claude, which links the case to his previous observations.

Rowley (Rolly) Claude visits Poirot to discuss his uncle, Gordon Claude, who married a widow named Andréa Deauville before his death. Rolly suspects that Enoch Yates is actually Robert Andeh, using an alias. He notes that the name "Enoch Yates" is a literary reference to a character who returns to find his wife remarried. Rolly explains that Beatrice Lippincott overheard a conversation suggesting Andeh was in the area under an alias to avoid trouble for David Hunter. The deceased man possessed no identification documents, only personal items like clothing and a toothbrush. Rolly also mentions that David Hunter, Rosalyn Claude's brother, claimed to have met the man and given him five pounds after receiving a letter stating the man was a friend of the supposedly destitute Robert Andeh.
